pattern: Two equal-speed walkers reach a fixed point first based purely on the absolute gap to it, so the answer is a direct comparison of two distances.
hints: Speed is identical for both movers, so arrival time depends only on how far each starts from the fixed target.
Distance on a number line is the absolute difference of positions; compare the two distances.
Compute |x - z| and |y - z|; return 1 if the first is smaller, 2 if the second is smaller, and 0 when they tie.

edge_inputs.description: person one already at the target
extreme positions at the range ends

stress.description: random small triples covering ties and both winners
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    x = rng.randint(1, 100)
    y = rng.randint(1, 100)
    z = rng.randint(1, 100)
    return [x, y, z]
stress.note: constants are clamped to the 1..100 range
